description_long: This is a long polearm weapon featuring a prominent, curved blade at one end and a pointed spear tip at the other. The blade is wide, single-edged, and has a slightly jagged, ornamental profile, suggesting a design inspired by traditional Chinese guandao or similar polearms. The shaft is long, straight, and appears to be made of dark metal or wood, reinforced with metallic bands and decorative fittings. The handle section is cylindrical and includes two circular, gold-toned guards or collars near each end, providing both structural support and visual contrast. The overall finish is matte with subtle metallic highlights, and the blade and fittings have a greenish tint, possibly indicating a patina or intentional coloration. There are no visible engravings or elaborate ornamentation, giving the weapon a functional yet slightly stylized appearance.
description: A long, double-ended polearm with a curved, greenish blade on one end and a pointed spear tip on the other, featuring a dark shaft with gold-toned fittings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the polearm, highlighting the curved blade and pointed spear tip.
description_view_1: End-on view emphasizing the narrow profile of the shaft and the circular guard near the blade.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, displaying the blade's curvature and the symmetry of the shaft and fittings.
description_view_3: End-on view from the spear tip, showing the alignment of the shaft and the circular guards.
attribute_blade_type: curved, single-edged, wide polearm blade with spear tip
attribute_handle_design: straight cylindrical shaft with metallic bands and circular guards, dark finish
